Klasse HandlingBulkData

java.lang.Object
de.btu.kt.syx.tutorials.HandlingBulkData
public class HandlingBulkData extends Object
Coding examples of handling bulk data.

[<prev] | Back to [Tutorials] | [next>]

Getting Started

  1. Fetching a Patch Name
  2. Creating a Simple Patch Bank
  3. Reading and Writing SysEx Files
Autor:
Matthias Wolff

    static void

    Demonstrates how to create simple a patch bank.

    Demonstrates how to fetch a patch name from a device.

  • Methodendetails

    • fetchPatchName

      public static void fetchPatchName()
      Demonstrates how to fetch a patch name from a device.

      [<prev] | Back to [Handling Bulk Data] [Tutorials] | [next>]

      Fetching a Patch Name

      We will (synchronously) fetch a patch name from a Yamaha TG55. You may rewrite the example for your own device as an exercise. The device must support patch bulk requests.

      We create, open and close the MIDI interface as explained in Getting Started/Creating a MIDI Interface.

        1  final int     devNum = 0; // <- Your zero-based device number here
  2  final String  miName = "KOMPLETE KONTROL EXT - 1"; // <- Name of your MIDI interface here
  3  MidiInterface mi     = null;
  4 
  5  try
  6  {
  7    mi = new MidiInterface(miName);
  8    mi.open();

      The first step is to create the TG55 voice bulk dump message we want to receive (see [1], pp. Add-6, 9-11). Fetching the voice bulk will be synchronized on this object.

        9    final SyxMessage blkDmp = new SyxMessage();

      Next we register a SysEx message listener with the MIDI interface:

       10    mi.addSysExListener(new ISysexMessageListener()
 11    {
 12 
 13      @Override
 14      public void receiveSysexMsg(SysexMessage sxMsg, long timeStamp)
 15      throws InvalidMidiDataException
 16      {

      Upon receiving a SysEx message, we first just print the raw data:

       17        rcvrLog.log(0,2,"SyxEx message received:\n");
 18        byte[] syxData = sxMsg.getMessage();
 19        rcvrLog.log(SYX.prettyPrintByteArray(syxData,""));
 20        rcvrLog.log(0,-2,"\n\n");

      (rcvrLog is an interface to SYX's global message logger supplied by class HandlingBulkData to ease console print-outs).

      Now we detect whether the received SysEx message is a TG55 bulk dump (of any type). To this end, we initially only parse the message header. If this fails, an InvalidMidiDataException will be thrown indicating to the SysEx message listener framework that our receiver does not understand and process the received message. This preliminary step is necessary because TG55 voice bulk messages, which we want to receive, contain a variable number of payload bytes depending on the voice settings. We need the payload length in preparation for parsing the voice bulk.

       21        try
 22        {
 23          SyxMessage rcvMsg = new SyxMessage();
 24          rcvMsg.addParts(new SyxDataStruct(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      TG55
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      F_BD_HEADER1
      

      
,"Bulk Header 1"));
 25          rcvMsg.setMessage(sxMsg.getMessage(),rcvMsg.getLength()/*Header bytes only!*/);
 26          rcvrLog.log(0,2,"TG55 bulk header 1 parsed successfully:\n");
 27          rcvrLog.log(rcvMsg.prettyPrint());
 28          rcvrLog.log(0,-2,"\n\n");

      The payload size is contained in parameter 'b' of the bulk header (see

      Ungültige Referenz 
      format specification
      and [1] p. Add-6):

       29          int bc = rcvMsg.getPart(0).getMidiValue('b');

      Now we're all set to actually parse the received voice bulk:

       30          rcvMsg.clear();
 31          rcvMsg.addParts(new SyxDataStruct(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      TG55
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      F_BD_HEADER1
      

      
 ,"Bulk Header 1"));
 32          rcvMsg.addParts(new SyxDataStruct(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      TG55
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      F_BD_HEADER2
      

      
 ,"Bulk Header 2"));
 33          rcvMsg.addParts(new SyxDataStruct(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      TG55
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      F_BDVC_HEADER
      

      
,"Voice Header" ));
 34          bc -= (rcvMsg.getPart(1).getLength() + rcvMsg.getPart(2).getLength());
 35          rcvMsg.addParts(new SyxDataStruct("**H ".repeat(bc) ,"(Other Data)"));
 36          rcvMsg.addParts(new SyxChecksum(SyxChecksum.ROLAND,1,-1));
 37          rcvMsg.setMessage(sxMsg.getMessage(),rcvMsg.getLength());

      In lines 31–36 we have created the entire voice bulk message structure. The part added in line 35 is a dummy just accepting all bytes of the voice bulk except the voice header (see line 33 and [1] p. Add-9) which contains the patch name. Only the size of the dummy part must be correct. Hence, we used the playload size obtained in line 29 to make its format specifier (lines 34, 35). In line 37 we parsed the received message. If this fails, an InvalidMidiDataException will be thrown, again indicating to the framework that our receiver does not understand and process this message.

      So far, so good. In a last step, we have to check the device number, to make sure we got the correct bulk type, and to validate the checksum: 

       38          int dn = rcvMsg.getPart(0).getMidiValue('#');
 39          if (dn!=devNum)
 40            throw SYX.InvMdataExc("Wrong devive no. %d; must be %d",dn,devNum);
 41          String bid = rcvMsg.getPart(1).readString("st");
 42          if (!"VC".equals(bid))
 43            throw SYX.InvMdataExc("Bad bulk ID '%s'; must be 'VC'",bid);
 44          rcvMsg.validateChecksum();
 45          rcvrLog.log("TG55 voice bulk dump parsed successfully\n");

      Finally, we synchronize on blkDmp, copy the parsed message into it, and notify the main thread of having successfully received a TG55 voice bulk:

       46          rcvrLog.log("-> Notifying MAIN\n\n");
 47          synchronized (blkDmp)
 48          {
 49            blkDmp.copy(rcvMsg);
 50            blkDmp.notifyAll();
 51          }
 52        }

      The try-catch block starting in line 21 is not essential to the function. It was just inserted to print any InvalidMidiDataExceptions that might have occurred. After that, we just re-throw the exception.

       53        catch (InvalidMidiDataException e)
 54        {
 55          rcvrLog.errlog("Not a TG55 voice bulk (%s)",e.getMessage());
 56          throw e;
 57        }
 58      }
 59
 60      @Override
 61      public int getDevNum()
 62      {
 63        return devNum;
 64      }
 65
 66    });

      Back in the main thread, we create a TG55 voice bulk request message: 

       67    SyxDataStruct prt = new SyxDataStruct(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      TG55
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      F_BULK_REQUEST
      

      
);
 68    prt.setMidiValue('#',devNum); // Device no.
 69    prt.setMidiValue('s','V'   ); // Voice bulk type
 70    prt.setMidiValue('t','C'   ); // Voice bulk type
 71    prt.setMidiValue('x',0x00  ); // Patch I01 (Bank no.)
 72    prt.setMidiValue('y',0x00  ); // Patch I01 (Patch no.)
 73    SyxMessage blkReq = new SyxMessage(prt);

      Then we clear the expected voice bulk dump, synchronize on it, send the bulk request, and wait for the bulk dump to arrive:

       74    long then = System.nanoTime();
 75    blkDmp.clear();
 76    synchronized (blkDmp)
 77    {
 78      mainLog.log("Requesting TG55 voice bulk dump (Patch I01)\n");
 79      mi.send(blkReq);
 80      try
 81      {
 82        mainLog.log("Waiting for SYX_RCVR\n\n");
 83        blkDmp.wait(2000);
 84      }
 85      catch (InterruptedException e)
 86      { // Ignore
 87      }
 88    }

      (mainLog is an interface to SYX's global message logger supplied by class HandlingBulkData to ease console print-outs).

      If we received a voice bulk dump in reply to the request, blkDmp will be non-empty. In this case, we read-out and print the patch name. If blkDmp is empty, the request timed out.

       89    if (!blkDmp.isEmpty())
 90    {
 91      SyxDataStruct vceHdr = blkDmp.getPart(2);
 92      String patchName = vceHdr.readString("a-j");
 93      mainLog.log("Patch I01 - '%s'\n",patchName);
 94      long now = System.nanoTime();
 95      mainLog.log("(Turnaround time: %.0f ms)\n",(now-then)/1000000.);
 96    }
 97    else
 98      mainLog.errlog("TIME OUT!\n");
 99   }
100  catch (Exception e)
101  {
102    e.printStackTrace();
103  }
104  finally
105  {
106    if (mi!=null)
107      mi.close();
108  }

      References:

      [1] Yamaha Corp.: TG55 Operating Manual. Online, retrieved May 12, 2026

    • createSimplePatchBank

      public static void createSimplePatchBank()
      Demonstrates how to create simple a patch bank.

      [<prev] | Back to [Handling Bulk Data] [Tutorials] | [next>]

      Creating a Simple Patch Bank

      We extend the previous example by fetching all patches from TG55 and collecting them in a patch bank. As most of the Java code is identical, we will only look at the modifications:

      1. We remove the lengthy console print-outs.

      2. Before line 5 in the previous example, we create value maps for the 'x' and 'y' parameters of
        Ungültige Referenz 
        bulk header #2
        (see [1], p. Add-6): 
         final int[]    bankNums  = new int[] {0x00, 0x02};
 final String[] bankIDs   = new String[] {"I","P"};
 final int[]    patchNums = SYX.getIntRange(0x00,0x3F);
 final String[] patchIDs  = new String[0x40];
 for (int i=0x00; i<=0x3F; i++)
   patchIDs[i] = String.format("%02d",i+1);
      3. At the same location we create a patch bank object: 
         BankTree<BankTree.IBankObject> patchBank
   = new BankTree<BankTree.IBankObject>("Patch","Patch Bank");
 patchBank.addBank(bankIDs[0],"Internal");
 patchBank.addBank(bankIDs[1],"Preset");
        In this example we create a simple patch bank which will only contain the patch names. Patch banks can also contain patch data (see
        Ungültige Referenz 
        VZ1PatchBankAppc
        for an advanced example).
      4. We replace line 32 in the SysEx message listener of the previous example by: 
         SyxDataStruct blkHdr2;
 SyxParamInfo pi;
 blkHdr2 = new SyxDataStruct(

        
Ungültige Referenz

        TG55
        

        
.

        
Ungültige Referenz

        F_BD_HEADER2
        

        
,"Bulk Header 2");
 pi = new SyxParamInfo(blkHdr2,'x',"BANK_ID","Bank ID");
 pi.setValueMap(bankNums,bankIDs);
 blkHdr2.addParamInfo(pi);
 pi = new SyxParamInfo(blkHdr2,'y',"PATCH_ID","Patch ID");
 pi.setValueMap(patchNums,patchIDs);
 blkHdr2.addParamInfo(pi);
 rcvMsg.addParts(blkHdr2);
        which assigns the value maps created in 2. to the parameters 'x' and 'y' in the second bulk header of the received bulk dump (see Getting Started/Adding Parameter Information). This allows us to retrieve plain-text bank and patch IDs (as shown on the synthesizer) from the voice bulks we are going to fetch.
      5. We replace lines 67–99 in the previous example by nested loops over the bank patch numbers from 2. 
         mainLog.log(0,2,"Fetching patch names");
 for (int bankNum : bankNums)
   for (int patchNum : patchNums)
   {
        Create voice bulk request for bank bankNum and patch patchNum: 
             SyxDataStruct prt = new SyxDataStruct(TG55FormatSpecifiers.F_TG55_BULK_REQUEST);
     prt.setMidiValue('#',devNum  ); // Device no.
     prt.setMidiValue('s','V'     ); // Voice bulk type
     prt.setMidiValue('t','C'     ); // Voice bulk type
     prt.setMidiValue('x',bankNum ); // Bank no.
     prt.setMidiValue('y',patchNum); // Patch no.
    SyxMessage blkReq = new SyxMessage(prt);
        Send bulk request and wait for voice bulk dump: 
             blkDmp.clear();
     synchronized (blkDmp)
     {
       mi.send(blkReq);
       try
       {
         blkDmp.wait(2000);
       }
       catch (InterruptedException e)
       { // Ignore
       }
     }
        If we received a bulk dump, read the bank and patch IDs, and the patch name. Then create a patch bank entry: 
             if (!blkDmp.isEmpty())
     {
       if (patchNum==0)
         mainLog.log("\n");
       mainLog.log(".");
 
       // Get and print patch name
       SyxDataStruct blkHdr2 = blkDmp.getPart(1);
       SyxDataStruct vceHdr  = blkDmp.getPart(2);
       String bankID    = blkHdr2.getModelValueAsString('x');
       String patchID   = blkHdr2.getModelValueAsString('y');
       String patchName = vceHdr.readString("a-j");
       patchBank.addBankObject(bankID,patchID,patchName);
     }
     else
       mainLog.errlog(".");
   }
   
 mainLog.log(0,-2,"\n");
 mainLog.log("Done\n\n");

 > MAIN    : Fetching patch names
 > MAIN    :   ................................................................
 > MAIN    :   ................................................................
 > MAIN    : Done
        Finally, we pretty-print the patch bank: 
         mainLog.log(patchBank.prettyPrint());
        On my TG55 it looks as follows: 
         > MAIN    : ------------------------------
 > MAIN    : Patch Bank      UID Name         
 > MAIN    : ------------------------------
 > MAIN    : Bank Tree     :     Patch Bank
 > MAIN    : |- Patch Bank : I   Internal
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 01  ZenAirBell
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 02  GenChorus 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 03  GB-Chorus 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 04  SP.PanPipe
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 05  INIT VOICE
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 06  SP:DreamPd
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 07  SC.Dazling
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 08  SP:Lonely 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 09  SP*Synfony
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 10  SE:Fredy  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 11  ME*SynWave
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 12  ~ A Bow  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 13  ClassPiano
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 14  Rock Piano
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 15  SE:"Hit!!"
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 16  ST:StrDust
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 17  GX Dream  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 18  HardAtckGX
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 19  Deep Organ
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 20  Warm Organ
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 21  Trumpet   
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 22  BA:Rezo   
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 23  Big Band  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 24  Orch Brass
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 25  SynthBrass
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 26  Flute     
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 27  Saxophone 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 28  FolkGuitar
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 29  12 String 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 30  MuteGuitar
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 31  SingleCoil
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 32  Pick Bass 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 33  Thumb Bass
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 34  SynBadBass
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 35  VCO Bass  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 36  Violin    
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 37  ChamberStr
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 38  VCF String
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 39  Nova Quire
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 40  Vibraphone
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 41  Takerimba 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 42  ShortBell 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 43  WarmBell  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 44  PC:ItoChim
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 45  VCO Lead  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 46  Spirit VCF
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 47  OZ Lead   
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 48  Get Lucky 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 49  Gamma Band
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 50  Metal Reed
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 51  Modomatic 
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 52  Gently    
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 53  Mystichoir
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 54  St.Michael
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 55  Sharpy    
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 56  FreeThem  
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 57  Hollow Pad
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 58  SatinGlass
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 59  SatinGlass
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 60  Saeg es   
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 61  Revelation
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 62  WdBass Duo
 > MAIN    : |- |- Patch   : 63  DR Electry
 > MAIN    : |- '- Patch   : 64  DR Dance  
 > MAIN    : '- Patch Bank : P   Preset
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 01  Piano     
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 02  Voyager   
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 03  Pro55Brass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 04  Elektrodes
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 05  Zaratustra
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 06  DawnChorus
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 07  GX Dream  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 08  GrooveKing
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 09  DistGuitar
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 10  ZenAirBell
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 11  FullString
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 12  Jazz Man  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 13  ClassPiano
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 14  Rock Piano
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 15  DX E.Piano
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 16  Hard EP   
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 17  Cry Clav  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 18  Funky Clav
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 19  Deep Organ
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 20  Warm Organ
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 21  Trumpet   
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 22  Stab Brass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 23  Big Band  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 24  Orch Brass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 25  SynthBrass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 26  Flute     
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 27  Saxophone 
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 28  FolkGuitar

 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 30  MuteGuitar
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 31  SingleCoil
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 32  Pick Bass 
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 33  Thumb Bass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 34  SynBadBass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 35  VCO Bass  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 36  Violin    
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 37  ChamberStr
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 38  VCF String
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 39  Nova Quire
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 40  Vibraphone
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 41  Takerimba 
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 42  Glocken   
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 43  DigiBell  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 44  Oriental  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 45  VCO Lead  
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 46  Spirit VCF
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 47  OZ Lead   
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 48  Get Lucky 
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 49  Gamma Band
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 50  Metal Reed
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 51  Modomatic 
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 52  DataStream
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 53  Mystichoir
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 54  St.Michael
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 55  Scatter   
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 56  Triton    
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 57  Amazon    
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 58  SatinGlass
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 59  BrassChime
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 60  Piano Mist
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 61  Xanadu    
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 62  WdBass Duo
 > MAIN    :   |- Patch    : 63  Drum Set 1
 > MAIN    :   '- Patch    : 64  Drum Set 2
 > MAIN    : ------------------------------
 > MAIN    : * Patch data available

      References:

      [1] Yamaha Corp.: TG55 Operating Manual. Online, retrieved May 12, 2026

    • syxFiles

      public static void syxFiles()
      Demonstrates reading and writing SYX files.

      [<prev] | Back to [Handling Bulk Data] [Tutorials] | [next>]

      Reading and Writing SysEx Files

      SYX provides a SysEx recoder which contains a "tape" whose "tracks" are SysEx messages. The tape can be recorded, played back, and written to or read from a SYX or TXT file.

      Note: This example requires the SYX.Devices package!

      Let's record a little SysEx tape:

       try
 {
   SysexRecorder recorder = new SysexRecorder();
   recorder.record(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      VZ1PatchAppc
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      vz1Patch_Karamoon
      

      
());
   recorder.record(

      
Ungültige Referenz

      VZ1PatchAppc
      

      
.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      vz1Patch_NothingToFear
      

      
());

      The tape now contains two demo patches of the Casio VZ-1/10M synthesizer.

      We save the patches into a temporary SYX file:

         File tmpFile = File.createTempFile("syxtape",".syx");
   tmpFile.deleteOnExit();
   String fileName = tmpFile.getAbsolutePath();
   recorder.writeSyxFile(fileName);

      Then we clear the tape and reload it from the SYX file:

         recorder.reset();
   recorder.readSyxFile(fileName);

      Now we play the reloaded tape back to a SysEx message listener which we attach to the recorder:

         recorder.addSysExListener(new ISysexMessageListener()
   {

     @Override
     public void receiveSysexMsg(SysexMessage sxMsg, long timeStamp)
     throws InvalidMidiDataException
     {
       

      
Ungültige Referenz

      VZ1Patch
      

      
 vz1Patch = new 

      
Ungültige Referenz

      VZ1Patch
      

      
(sxMsg.getData());
       System.out.println(vz1Patch.

      
Ungültige Referenz

      prettyPrintModel
      

      
());
     }

     @Override
     public int getDevNum()
     { // Not needed
       return 0;
     }

   });
   recorder.play();
 }
 catch (Exception e)
 {
   e.printStackTrace();
 }

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
